research.status_summary: Active layoffs in progress as of May 20, 2026 — Meta cut ~90–100 employees (10% of ~900 Israeli headcount) at its Tel Aviv R&D center as part of a global 8,000-person reduction, while simultaneously shifting 7,000 employees globally into AI roles.
research.hiring_policy: Meta Tel Aviv hires directly as a local legal entity (no EOR needed); roles listed on LinkedIn as 'Actively Hiring' include AI Research Scientist, Data Scientist – Product Analytics, ML Engineers, and Software Engineering Managers as of June 2026. No public EOR or remote-first policy for TLV roles — positions are listed as on-site or hybrid in Tel Aviv.
recent_news: May 20, 2026: ~90–100 Meta Israel employees received dismissal notices — 10% of local ~900-person workforce — primarily from the Tel Aviv R&D/development center, mirroring a global 8,000-job cut (Times of Israel, Globes, Calcalist, May 2026).

April 2026: Meta announced a Reality Labs hardware reorganization, moving QA/dogfooding and platform foundations teams directly into wearables and VR units to 'adapt and execute faster,' per a memo from Applied AI Engineering head Maher Saba (PYMNTS, April 15, 2026).

Meta's FAIR (Fundamental AI Research) division — which has a Tel Aviv workspace — was folded under the newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs (MSL), which expanded to 180 researchers across Menlo Park, London, and Tel Aviv, targeting AGI development (Wikipedia/FourWeekMBA, 2026).
A second layoff wave of ~10% is anticipated in August–September 2026, ahead of Meta's annual Connect conference, per Globes/Bloomberg estimates.
research.growth_signal: Despite layoffs, Meta is on an aggressive AI infrastructure trajectory — committing $100B+ in capex in 2026, signing a $100B multi-year GPU deal with AMD, and growing its Tel Aviv AI Research and Reality Labs teams as the R&D center remains one of Meta's largest strategic development hubs outside the US.
research.fit_for_lior: Strong fit for Lior's Creative AI and Product Analyst threads — Tel Aviv roles actively open in Data Scientist (Product Analytics), Funnel Personalization PM, and AI Research Scientist align directly with her profile, though the 10% layoff wave signals a cautious entry window and a preference for AI-native candidates.
